subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – {DEPT} Contracts Over {AMOUNT}
body_tmpl: Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I am requesting copies of all contracts awarded by the {DEPT} department in excess of {AMOUNT} during the period {DATE} through present. Please include vendor names, contract amounts, scope of work, and any sole-source justification memos. I am a journalist with {BIZ} and this request is for news-gathering purposes, which qualifies for a fee waiver under 5 ILCS 140/6. Please respond within five business days as required by statute. If any records will be withheld, please provide the specific statutory exemption cited for each.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your FOIA request received {DATE}. The Village of Bellwood acknowledges receipt and will respond on or before the statutory deadline of {DAYS} business days. If we require an extension, you will be notified in writing. Please reference case number {CASE} in all future correspondence.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Police Body Camera Footage {DATE}
body_tmpl: I am submitting this FOIA request under 5 ILCS 140 for all body-worn camera footage recorded by Bellwood Police Department officers responding to the incident at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} at approximately {TIME}. I am the attorney of record for {FIRST} {LAST}, who was a party to the incident. Please provide footage in its original digital format. If any portion is withheld under the Law Enforcement Officer-Worn Body Camera Act, please identify each exemption applied. I expect a response within five business days.
reply_tmpl: Your request (Case {CASE}) has been forwarded to the Police Department records custodian. Body camera materials are subject to additional review under the Body Camera Act; we will notify you within the statutory period if an extension is necessary. You may be contacted at {PHONE} if clarification is needed.

subject_tmpl: Illinois FOIA – Employee Salary Records {DEPT}
body_tmpl: Under the Illinois FOIA (5 ILCS 140/2), I request the following public records: a complete list of all Village employees in the {DEPT} department as of {DATE}, including job title, base salary, overtime paid in the prior fiscal year, and total compensation. Salary records for public employees are explicitly public under Illinois law. I am a researcher at {BIZ}. Please provide records in electronic spreadsheet format if available, which will reduce copying costs. The five-business-day response clock began upon receipt of this email.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Code Enforcement Records {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: This is a formal FOIA request under 5 ILCS 140 for all code enforcement complaints, inspection reports, violation notices, and correspondence related to the property at {ADDRESS}, Bellwood, IL, for the period {DATE} to present. I am {FIRST} {LAST}, a resident and adjacent property owner at {STREET}. I believe there are open violations affecting my property value and I am entitled to these records. Please provide copies in PDF format. I waive duplication fees for the first 50 pages per statute.
reply_tmpl: Ms./Mr. {LAST}, your request (Case {CASE}) has been received by the Village Clerk's office. Records from Code Enforcement will be compiled and provided within five business days. If the volume of records requires an extension, you will be notified in writing with an expected completion date.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Village Email Records Regarding {STREET} Project
body_tmpl: Pursuant to the Illinois FOIA, I hereby request all emails, text messages, and electronic communications between any Village employee and {BIZ} (or its principals) concerning the {STREET} infrastructure project, from {DATE} through the present date. This includes communications sent from personal devices if they pertain to Village business. I am a watchdog journalist. I am not affiliated with any commercial enterprise and request a fee waiver. Please confirm receipt and provide the assigned case number.
reply_tmpl: Your FOIA request has been logged under Case {CASE} and assigned to the appropriate department heads for record retrieval. Email searches across multiple departments may require the full statutory period. We will provide responsive records or a lawful denial notice on or before {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Public Records Request – Stormwater Grant Applications
body_tmpl: I am writing to request, under the Illinois FOIA, copies of all grant applications submitted by the Village of Bellwood to any state or federal agency for stormwater infrastructure improvements during the last three fiscal years. Please include the application, any award letters or denial notices, and any engineering reports submitted as attachments. I represent {BIZ}, a nonprofit focused on municipal infrastructure transparency. Electronic copies are preferred. The five-day statutory clock applies from the date of this email.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Permits Issued at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: This FOIA request seeks all building permits, certificates of occupancy, and inspection sign-off records for the property located at {ADDRESS} in Bellwood since {DATE}. I am a prospective buyer conducting due diligence and understand these records are public. Please include any open or lapsed permits and any stop-work orders. If records can be provided electronically, I would appreciate that option to avoid copying fees. My contact number is {PHONE} if you need to reach me.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for contacting the Village of Bellwood. Your permit-history request for {ADDRESS} (Case {CASE}) has been forwarded to the Building Department. Records will be compiled within five business days. You will receive an email with instructions for any applicable copying fees before documents are released.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Vendor Payments to {BIZ}
body_tmpl: Under 5 ILCS 140, I am requesting all purchase orders, payment vouchers, and check registers showing payments made by the Village of Bellwood to {BIZ} for the fiscal years {DATE} through present. I am an investigative reporter with {NAME} Media. This request qualifies for a fee waiver under the news-gathering exemption. Please provide records in electronic format. If any payments are held in a TIF fund or special account, those should be included. I expect a response within the statutory five-business-day window.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request from Resident – Police Incident Report {DATE}
body_tmpl: Hi, my name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {ADDRESS}. I am requesting a copy of the police incident report from {DATE} involving a vehicle accident at the intersection of {STREET} and {CROSS}. I was involved as a witness. I understand this falls under the Illinois FOIA and that a small copying fee may apply. Please let me know the total cost before sending. You can reach me at {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, your FOIA request (Case {CASE}) has been received. Police Department records are being retrieved. The copying fee for your request is estimated at {AMOUNT}. Please reply to this email to confirm you authorize the fee and we will process your request promptly.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – 911 Call Records {DATE}
body_tmpl: Pursuant to the Illinois FOIA and the Illinois State Records Act, I am requesting copies of all 911 call logs, CAD dispatch records, and audio recordings for calls received between {TIME} and one hour later on {DATE} relating to the address {ADDRESS}. I am a licensed attorney representing a client in pending civil litigation. Please note that I am not requesting information protected by ongoing criminal investigation exemptions, and any such exemption should be documented specifically. The five-day statutory response period applies.

subject_tmpl: Illinois FOIA – Public Works Bid Documents {STREET}
body_tmpl: This is a FOIA request for all bid solicitations, submitted bids, bid tabulation sheets, and award documentation for the {STREET} road resurfacing project advertised in {DATE}. I represent {BIZ}, a contractor that submitted a bid and believes the award process may not have followed competitive bidding requirements. If any documents are withheld, please specify the statutory exemption. I expect a complete response within five business days of receipt.
reply_tmpl: Your FOIA request (Case {CASE}) regarding the {STREET} project has been received and forwarded to the Public Works and Finance departments. We will compile the requested bid documents and respond by {DATE}. If records are voluminous, we will notify you of any extension within the statutory deadline.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – TIF District Expenditure Reports
body_tmpl: I am requesting, under the Illinois FOIA, copies of all annual TIF district reports submitted to the Illinois Comptroller by the Village of Bellwood for the most recent five years, as well as any internal expenditure ledgers or project lists associated with each TIF fund. I am a community organizer with {BIZ}, which advocates for TIF transparency in Cook County. I request a fee waiver on the basis that this disclosure benefits the public. Please provide records electronically.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Fire Department Incident Reports {DATE}
body_tmpl: Under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, I request all fire department incident reports and run sheets for calls responded to on {DATE} and {DAYS} days surrounding that date at the property located at {ADDRESS}. I am {FIRST} {LAST}, an insurance adjuster handling a structural loss claim. Please include any arson investigation referral documentation. If records are not available for immediate release due to pending investigation, please provide a written explanation citing the applicable statutory exemption.
reply_tmpl: Mr./Ms. {LAST}, your request (Case {CASE}) has been forwarded to the Fire Shift Commander for records compilation. Fire incident reports are generally releasable unless an investigation is active. You will receive confirmation of status and any applicable fees within five business days.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Liquor License Applications {BIZ}
body_tmpl: This is a formal FOIA request for all liquor license applications, renewals, inspection reports, and any complaints or suspension actions on file for {BIZ} located at {ADDRESS} for the period {DATE} to present. I am a neighbor and believe the establishment has operated outside licensed hours. If there are any administrative hearing records, please include those as well. I understand the five-business-day response window and ask that documents be emailed to me in PDF format.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Water Main Break History {STREET}
body_tmpl: Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140, I am requesting the following records: all water main break reports, repair work orders, and associated cost records for {STREET} between {CROSS} and the next intersection, for the five years preceding {DATE}. I am {NAME}, a property owner at {ADDRESS} who has experienced repeated basement flooding and believes deteriorating infrastructure is a contributing cause. I am not requesting a fee waiver and understand standard copying costs apply.
reply_tmpl: Your FOIA request (Case {CASE}) has been assigned to the Public Works and Water departments. Infrastructure maintenance records will be compiled and provided within five business days. If the scope requires additional time, you will receive written notice. Thank you for reaching out, {FIRST}.

subject_tmpl: Press Inquiry + FOIA – Mayor's Office Calendar {DATE}
body_tmpl: I am a reporter with {BIZ} covering Cook County municipal government. I am submitting a dual press inquiry and formal FOIA request for the Mayor's official public calendar and any meeting logs for the month of {DATE}. I am specifically interested in any meetings with representatives of {NAME} or affiliated developers. Please treat this as a FOIA request under 5 ILCS 140 if a press inquiry is not fulfilled within 48 hours. Fee waiver requested on news-gathering grounds.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your inquiry. The Mayor's Office will coordinate with the Village Clerk on the FOIA component (Case {CASE}). For press scheduling, please contact Tina Alvarez in the Mayor's Office at Village Hall. We aim to respond to press inquiries promptly in addition to meeting the statutory FOIA deadline.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Village Council Meeting Minutes {DATE} Through {DATE}
body_tmpl: I am requesting certified copies of all Village Board meeting minutes, including closed session minutes to the extent required to be released under 5 ILCS 120/2.06, from {DATE} through {DATE}. I am a graduate researcher at {BIZ} studying municipal governance in Cook County. I also request any audio or video recordings of public sessions if they were made. Please provide records electronically. I request a fee waiver based on academic and public-interest grounds.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Inspection Records for {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Please accept this as a formal FOIA request for all building, electrical, plumbing, and health inspection records for the commercial property at {ADDRESS} from {DATE} to the present. I am the new owner of this property and need a complete compliance history before taking occupancy. I am happy to pay standard copy fees. Please email a fee estimate before releasing documents. My contact information is {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Welcome to Bellwood, {FIRST}. Your request (Case {CASE}) has been forwarded to the Building Department and Health Officer. Inspection histories for commercial properties are available as public records. We will compile documents and provide a fee estimate within five business days.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Parking Ticket Revenue and Collection Data {DATE}
body_tmpl: Under the Illinois FOIA, I request a data summary or database export showing the total number of parking citations issued by Bellwood PD each month from {DATE} through present, total fines assessed, total fines collected, and the name of any third-party collection agency under contract. I am a data journalist at {BIZ}. I request the data in CSV or Excel format if available, and a fee waiver on news-gathering grounds. Please acknowledge receipt and provide a case number.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Demolition Permits and Bids {STREET}
body_tmpl: This is a FOIA request for all demolition permits, bid solicitations, contractor proposals, and insurance certificates related to the demolition of the structure at {ADDRESS} on or about {DATE}. I am {NAME}, a certified building contractor who did not receive a solicitation for this project and is reviewing whether proper procurement was followed. I am also requesting the total amount paid for the demolition and the identity of the contractor awarded the work.
reply_tmpl: Your FOIA request (Case {CASE}) regarding demolition activities at {ADDRESS} has been received and forwarded to the Building Department and Finance Office. Records will be compiled within five business days. You will be notified if a statutory extension is required.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Drug and Narcotics Arrest Statistics {DATE}
body_tmpl: Under the Illinois FOIA, I am requesting aggregate statistical data — not individual records — on narcotics and drug-related arrests made by the Bellwood Police Department from {DATE} through {DATE}. Data requested: total arrests by charge type, race and age of arrestee (aggregate only), and geographic distribution by beat or ward. I represent {BIZ}, a public health research organization. No individual identifying information is sought. Please provide data in tabular electronic format.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Snow Removal Contracts and Costs {DATE}
body_tmpl: Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140, I am requesting: all contracts with private snow removal vendors for the {DATE} winter season, total expenditure on salt and de-icing materials, and any after-action reports on snow clearing performance. I am {FIRST} {LAST}, a resident of {STREET} who experienced delayed plowing on multiple occasions this winter. I believe the public interest is served by transparency in how these contracts are awarded and performed.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, thank you for your FOIA request (Case {CASE}). Winter operations records have been requested from Public Works. You will receive the responsive documents or a written response within five business days. We appreciate your patience.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Records of Nuisance Abatement Orders {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: I am formally requesting under the Illinois FOIA all nuisance abatement orders, notices of violation, administrative hearing records, and contractor invoices for remediation work performed at or billed to {ADDRESS} from {DATE} to present. I am the property heir following the death of the prior owner and need to understand any outstanding municipal liens. Please also include any lien certifications filed with Cook County. My attorney is {NAME} and this request is made on my behalf.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Business License Records for {STREET} Corridor
body_tmpl: On behalf of {BIZ}, I am submitting a FOIA request for a complete list of all current and lapsed business licenses issued to establishments operating along {STREET} between {CROSS} and {NUM} as of {DATE}. We are a commercial real estate firm conducting a market study. Records requested include business name, license type, issuance and expiration date, and license status. We are willing to pay applicable copying fees. Please confirm receipt and provide a case number.
reply_tmpl: Your FOIA request (Case {CASE}) from {BIZ} has been received. Business license records are available as public documents. The Village Clerk will compile and provide the requested list within five business days. A fee estimate will be provided if the copying cost exceeds {AMOUNT}.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Overtime Payments to {DEPT} Personnel {DATE}
body_tmpl: Under 5 ILCS 140, I request a spreadsheet listing all overtime compensation paid to employees in the {DEPT} department for the fiscal year ending {DATE}. Please include: employee name, job classification, regular hours, overtime hours, and total overtime paid. I am a fiscal watchdog affiliated with {BIZ}. Illinois courts have upheld that individual employee names and salaries are public records. Please do not redact names on that basis. I expect a response within five business days.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Environmental Inspection Reports Near {STREET}
body_tmpl: I am requesting under the Illinois FOIA any environmental inspection reports, soil testing records, or hazardous materials assessments conducted on Village-owned or Village-managed properties within three blocks of {STREET} and {CROSS}. I represent {BIZ}, a community environmental health organization. The request covers records from {DATE} to present. If the Village does not hold responsive records, please indicate which state or county agency may have jurisdiction so we can direct further requests.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your request (Case {CASE}). Environmental records at the municipal level are limited; we will consult with the Village Engineer and Health Officer to compile any responsive documents within five business days. We will advise if records are held by IEPA or Cook County and provide referral contact information.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Complaint History for My Block {STREET}
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. I would like to request under FOIA all service requests, complaints, and code enforcement actions logged for properties in the {NUM} block of {STREET} for the last two years. I'm a block club president and we're preparing a presentation for the Village Board about persistent quality-of-life issues on our block. I'm not looking for anyone's personal information — just the type and date of each reported issue. Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, thank you for reaching out. Your FOIA request (Case {CASE}) is in process. We will redact any personal identifying information from third-party complainants as required by statute, but all other complaint data for your block will be provided. We'll be in touch within five business days.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Sidewalk Repair Program Records {DATE}
body_tmpl: Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, I request: the complete list of properties selected for sidewalk repair or replacement under the Village's {DATE} capital improvement program, the criteria used for prioritization, total program budget, and all payments to contractors. I am a reporter for {BIZ} investigating whether the selection process is equitable across Village wards. I request a fee waiver on news-gathering grounds and electronic delivery of all records.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – All Records Related to {STREET} Road Project Complaints
body_tmpl: I am filing this FOIA request for all resident complaints, emails, letters, and petitions received by the Village concerning the {STREET} reconstruction project, from {DATE} through present. I am also requesting any internal communications between Village staff or officials discussing the handling of those complaints. I am {NAME}, a community advocate. I understand that some internal deliberative materials may be exempt, but please provide a specific exemption citation for any withheld records rather than a blanket denial.
reply_tmpl: Your request (Case {CASE}) has been received. Resident complaint records related to the {STREET} project will be compiled from multiple departments. Any internal deliberative communications that qualify for exemption under 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(f) will be identified in a redaction log provided with your response.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Park District Grant and Expenditure Records
body_tmpl: Under the Illinois FOIA, I am requesting copies of all grant agreements, disbursement records, and final reports associated with state or federal grants received for Bellwood park improvements from {DATE} to present. I am {FIRST} {LAST}, a parks advocate and resident of {STREET}. I am particularly interested in any OSLAD or ITEP grants and how the funds were spent. Electronic delivery preferred. Please provide a case number upon receipt.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Police Use-of-Force Reports {DATE} Through {DATE}
body_tmpl: This is a formal FOIA request for all use-of-force reports and related internal review documentation generated by the Bellwood Police Department from {DATE} through {DATE}. I am a policy researcher with {BIZ}. Please redact officer names if required by the applicable exemption but provide all other data fields, including incident date, type of force, subject demographic information (aggregate), and disciplinary outcome if any. I request a fee waiver on public-interest research grounds.
reply_tmpl: Your FOIA request (Case {CASE}) for use-of-force reports has been received and forwarded to the Police Department. These records are subject to review under 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c) for privacy exemptions. We will provide responsive, non-exempt records within five business days or notify you of any required extension.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Bellwood Budget Documents FY {DATE}
body_tmpl: I am requesting under the Illinois FOIA the adopted annual budget, any budget amendments, and the most recent audited financial statements for the Village of Bellwood for fiscal year {DATE}. I am {NAME} of {BIZ}, a civic finance watchdog group. I am also requesting any five-year capital improvement plan documents currently in effect. These documents are typically public; please provide them electronically. If any portion is claimed exempt, please explain specifically.

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Health Inspection Reports {BIZ}
body_tmpl: Under 5 ILCS 140, I request all food establishment health inspection reports for {BIZ} located at {ADDRESS} for the period {DATE} to present. I am a freelance food safety journalist. Health inspection records are widely recognized as public documents. Please include re-inspection reports and any closure orders or corrective action letters. I request electronic delivery and a fee waiver on news-gathering grounds. The five-day clock is running from receipt of this message.
reply_tmpl: Your request (Case {CASE}) has been forwarded to the Village Health Officer. Food inspection reports for licensed establishments are public records. Records will be provided within five business days. If any inspection is subject to an active enforcement action that would delay release, you will be notified in writing.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Special Event Permit Applications and Insurance Certificates
body_tmpl: I am submitting a FOIA request for all special event permit applications, approvals or denials, insurance certificates, and indemnification agreements filed with the Village of Bellwood for events held on public property or public rights-of-way from {DATE} through {DATE}. I am {FIRST} {LAST}, an event producer researching the permit process prior to submitting my own application for a proposed event at {STREET}. Understanding prior approvals will help me structure my application appropriately.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Towing Contracts and Impound Lot Records {DATE}
body_tmpl: Under the Illinois FOIA, I request the current towing and impound contract between the Village of Bellwood and any towing service provider, including all amendments and rate schedules, as well as aggregate data on vehicles towed and impound fees collected from {DATE} to present. I am an attorney with {BIZ} representing clients who have disputed towing fees. I also request any complaints filed against the towing contractor and the Village's responses to those complaints.
reply_tmpl: Your FOIA request (Case {CASE}) regarding towing contracts and impound data has been received. Records will be compiled from the Police Department, Finance Office, and the Village Clerk. You will receive a complete response or an extension notice within five business days.
